      have h₉ : ContDiffOn ℝ (⊤ : ℕ∞) (fun z : ℂ => φ₀'' ( (-1) / z)) (upperHalfPlaneSet : Set ℂ) := h₇
      have h₁₀ : ContDiffOn ℝ (⊤ : ℕ∞) (fun z : ℂ => Complex.exp (↑r * z)) (upperHalfPlaneSet : Set ℂ) := h₄
      have h₁₁ : ContDiffOn ℝ (⊤ : ℕ∞) (fun z : ℂ => (z ^ 2 : ℂ)) (upperHalfPlaneSet : Set ℂ) := h₅
      have h₁₂ : ContDiffOn ℝ (⊤ : ℕ∞) (fun z : ℂ => φ₀'' ( (-1) / z) * Complex.exp (↑r * z)) (upperHalfPlaneSet : Set ℂ) := by
        apply ContDiffOn.mul h₉ h₁₀

have h1 : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₅' r) ℍ₀ := by
    -- Use the fact that Φ₅' r is complex smooth to deduce it is real smooth
    -- This is because complex smoothness implies real smoothness
    have h2 : ContDiffOn 𝕜 ∞ (Φ₅' r) ℍ₀ := Φ₅'_contDiffOn_ℂ
    -- Use the fact that complex smoothness implies real smoothness
    have h3 : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₅' r) ℍ₀ := by
      -- Convert the complex smoothness to real smoothness
      convert h2.smoothness_of_complex_smoothness using 1 <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff]
      <;> norm_num
      <;> aesop
    exact h3
  exact h1

have h : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₅' r) ℍ₀ := by
    -- Use the fact that Φ₅' r is complex holomorphic and the real part of a holomorphic function is smooth
    have h₁ : ContDiffOn ℂ ∞ (Φ₅' r) ℍ₀ := Φ₅'_contDiffOn_ℂ
    -- Convert the complex smoothness to real smoothness
    have h₂ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₅' r) ℍ₀ := by
      -- Use the fact that the real part of a holomorphic function is smooth
      apply Complex.contDiffOn_iff_contDiffOn_real_part.1 h₁
    exact h₂
  exact h

have h : ContDiffOn ℝ ⊤ (Φ₅' r) upperHalfPlaneSet := by
    have h₁ : ContDiffOn ℂ ⊤ (Φ₅' r) upperHalfPlaneSet := by
      apply Φ₅'_holo.contDiffOn
      exact isOpen_upperHalfPlaneSet
    -- Use the fact that complex differentiability implies real differentiability
    have h₂ : ContDiffOn ℝ ⊤ (Φ₅' r) upperHalfPlaneSet :=
      (ComplexComplex.contDiffOn_iff_realContDiffOn_re_IM (open_of_mem_nhds (isOpen_upperHalfPlaneSet.mem_nhds (by
        use 1 + 1 * Complex.I
        simp [Complex.ext_iff, upperHalfPlaneSet]
        <;> norm_num
      ))).mp h₁)
    exact h₂
  -- Since `⊤` implies any `n`, we have the result for `n = ∞`
  have h₃ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₅' r) upperHalfPlaneSet := by
    have h₄ : ContDiffOn ℝ ⊤ (Φ₅' r) upperHalfPlaneSet := h
    have h₅ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₅' r) upperHalfPlaneSet :=
      (show (⊤ : ℕ∞) ≥ ∞ by simp [le_top]) |>.mono h₄
    exact h₅
  exact h₃

end ContDiffOn_Real
simpa [contDiffOn_const] using Φ₅'_contDiffOn_ℂ.of_complex

have h₁ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (Complex.exp (↑(-r) * z) : ℂ)) ℍ₀ := by
    apply Complex.contDiffOn_exp.comp _ isOpen_upperHalfPlaneSet
    apply contDiffOn_const.mul contDiffOn_id
  have h₂ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (z.re : ℂ)) ℍ₀ := by
    apply contDiffOn_id.re
  have h₃ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (z.im : ℂ)) ℍ₀ := by
    apply contDiffOn_id.im
  have h₄ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (φ₀'' (z) : ℂ)) ℍ₀ := by
    have h₄₁ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (φ₀'' : ℂ → ℂ) ℍ₀ := by
      have h₄₂ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (φ₀'' : ℂ → ℂ) (Set.univ : Set ℂ) := by
        -- Since φ₀'' is a complex polynomial, it is smooth everywhere.
        have h₄₃ : ContDiff ℝ ⊤ (φ₀'' : ℂ → ℂ) := by
          -- Prove that φ₀'' is smooth as a function from ℂ to ℂ.
          have h₄₄ : ContDiff ℝ ⊤ (fun z : ℂ => (φ₀'' z : ℂ)) := by
            -- Use the fact that φ₀'' is a polynomial in z and conjugate z.
            -- Since the polynomial is finite, it is smooth.
            apply contDiff_id.pow
              |>.add (contDiff_id.mul contDiff_const)
              |>.add contDiff_const
          exact h₄₄
        -- Since φ₀'' is smooth on ℝ, it is smooth on any subset of ℝ.
        exact h₄₃.contDiffOn
      -- Since φ₀'' is smooth on the entire complex plane, it is smooth on ℍ₀.
      exact h₄₂.mono (by intro z hz; trivial)
    exact h₄₁
  
  have h₅ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₅' r) ℍ₀ := by
    have h₅₁ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (z : ℂ)) ℍ₀ := by
      apply contDiffOn_id
    have h₅₂ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (φ₀'' (z) : ℂ)) ℍ₀ := h₄
    have h₅₃ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (Complex.exp (↑(-r) * z) : ℂ)) ℍ₀ := h₁
    have h₅₄ : (Φ₅' r) = (fun z : ℂ => (φ₀'' (-1 / z) : ℂ) * (Complex.exp (↑(-r) * z) : ℂ)) := by
      ext z
      simp [Φ₅']
      <;> ring_nf
      <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, pow_two]
      <;> field_simp
      <;> ring_nf
      <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, pow_two]
      <;> norm_num
      <;> linarith
    rw [h₅₄]
    -- Use the fact that the product of smooth functions is smooth.
    have h₅₅ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (φ₀'' (-1 / z) : ℂ)) ℍ₀ := by
      -- Prove that the function z ↦ -1/z is smooth on ℍ₀.
      have h₅₅₁ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (-1 / z : ℂ)) ℍ₀ := by
        -- Use the fact that the quotient of smooth functions is smooth if the denominator is non-zero.
        have h₅₅₂ : ContDiffOn ℝ ⊤ (fun z : ℂ => (-1 : ℂ) : ℂ → ℂ) ℍ₀ := by
          apply contDiffOn_const
        have h₅₅₃ : ContDiffOn ℝ ⊤ (fun z : ℂ => (z : ℂ)) ℍ₀ := by
          apply contDiffOn_id
        have h₅₅₄ : ContDiffOn ℝ ⊤ (fun z : ℂ => (-1 / z : ℂ)) ℍ₀ := by
          apply (h₅₅₂.div h₅₅₃)
          intro z hz
          -- Prove that the denominator is non-zero on ℍ₀.
          have h₅₅₅ : z ≠ 0 := by
            have h₅₅₆ : z.im > 0 := hz
            by_contra h
            have h₅₅₇ : z = 0 := by simpa using h
            have h₅₅₈ : z.im = 0 := by simp [h₅₅₇]
            linarith
          simp_all [Complex.ext_iff]
          <;> norm_num at *
          <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff]
          <;> norm_num at *
          <;> linarith
        exact h₅₅₄
      -- Compose the smooth function φ₀'' with the smooth function z ↦ -1/z.
      have h₅₅₅ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (φ₀'' : ℂ → ℂ) (Set.univ : Set ℂ) := by
        -- Since φ₀'' is a polynomial, it is smooth everywhere.
        have h₅₅₆ : ContDiff ℝ ⊤ (φ₀'' : ℂ → ℂ) := by
          -- Prove that φ₀'' is smooth as a function from ℂ to ℂ.
          have h₅₅₇ : ContDiff ℝ ⊤ (fun z : ℂ => (φ₀'' z : ℂ)) := by
            -- Use the fact that φ₀'' is a polynomial in z and conjugate z.
            -- Since the polynomial is finite, it is smooth.
            apply contDiff_id.pow
              |>.add (contDiff_id.mul contDiff_const)
              |>.add contDiff_const
          exact h₅₅₇
        -- Since φ₀'' is smooth on ℝ, it is smooth on any subset of ℝ.
        exact h₅₅₆.contDiffOn
      -- Since φ₀'' is smooth on the entire complex plane, it is smooth on the image of the smooth function z ↦ -1/z.
      have h₅₅₆ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (φ₀'' : ℂ → ℂ) (Set.range fun z : ℂ => (-1 / z : ℂ)) := by
        apply h₅₅₅.mono
        intro z hz
        simp_all [Set.mem_range]
        <;>
        (try
          {
            use 0
            <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff]
            <;> norm_num at *
            <;> linarith
          })
        <;>
        (try
          {
            use 1
            <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff]
            <;> norm_num at *
            <;> linarith
          })
      -- Since the composition of smooth functions is smooth, z ↦ φ₀''(-1/z) is smooth on ℍ₀.
      have h₅₅₇ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (φ₀'' (-1 / z) : ℂ)) ℍ₀ := by
        have h₅₅₈ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (-1 / z : ℂ)) ℍ₀ := h₅₅₁
        have h₅₅₉ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (φ₀'' : ℂ → ℂ) (Set.range fun z : ℂ => (-1 / z : ℂ)) := h₅₅₆
        -- Check that the image of ℍ₀ under z ↦ -1/z is contained in the domain where φ₀'' is smooth.
        have h₅₅₁₀ : (fun z : ℂ => (-1 / z : ℂ)) '' ℍ₀ ⊆ Set.range (fun z : ℂ => (-1 / z : ℂ)) := by
          intro w hw
          obtain ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩ := hw
          exact ⟨z, by simp⟩
        -- Use the fact that the composition of smooth functions is smooth.
        have h₅₅₁₁ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (φ₀'' : ℂ → ℂ) ((fun z : ℂ => (-1 / z : ℂ)) '' ℍ₀) := by
          apply h₅₅₆.mono
          exact h₅₅₁₀
        -- The composition of smooth functions is smooth.
        have h₅₅₁₂ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (φ₀'' (-1 / z) : ℂ)) ℍ₀ := by
          apply h₅₅₁₁.comp h₅₅₈
          <;> intro z hz <;> simp_all [Set.mem_range] <;>
            (try { use 0 <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff] <;> norm_num at * <;> linarith }) <;>
            (try { use 1 <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff] <;> norm_num at * <;> linarith })
        exact h₅₅₁₂
      exact h₅₅₇
    -- Use the fact that the product of smooth functions is smooth.
    have h₅₆ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (φ₀'' (-1 / z) : ℂ) * (Complex.exp (↑(-r) * z) : ℂ)) ℍ₀ := by
      apply ContDiffOn.mul h₅₅ h₅₃
    exact h₅₆
  exact h₅

have h₁ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => φ₀'' z) ℍ₀ := by
    -- Step 1: Prove that φ₀'' is C^∞ on the upper half-plane
    have h₂ : φ₀'' = fun z : ℂ => 1 / (1 + Complex.exp (-2 * π * Complex.I * (z : ℂ))) := by
      funext z
      simp [φ₀'']
      <;> ring_nf
      <;> simp [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.exp_re, Complex.exp_im]
      <;> norm_num
      <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.exp_re, Complex.exp_im]
      <;> field_simp [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.exp_re, Complex.exp_im]
      <;> ring_nf
      <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.exp_re, Complex.exp_im]
      <;> norm_num
      <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.exp_re, Complex.exp_im]
    rw [h₂]
    -- Use the fact that 1/(1 + exp(-2πi z)) is C^∞ on the upper half-plane
    -- This is a known result from complex analysis
    have h₃ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => 1 / (1 + Complex.exp (-2 * π * Complex.I * (z : ℂ)))) ℍ₀ := by
      -- Use the fact that the denominator is never zero in the upper half-plane
      -- and the function is smooth there
      have h₄ : ∀ z ∈ ℍ₀, (1 + Complex.exp (-2 * π * Complex.I * (z : ℂ)) : ℂ) ≠ 0 := by
        intro z hz
        have h₅ : z.im > 0 := hz
        have h₆ : Complex.exp (-2 * π * Complex.I * (z : ℂ)) ≠ -1 := by
          -- Prove that exp(-2πi z) ≠ -1 for z in the upper half-plane
          have h₇ : Complex.exp (-2 * π * Complex.I * (z : ℂ)) = Complex.exp (-2 * π * Complex.I * (z : ℂ)) := rfl
          have h₈ : Complex.exp (-2 * π * Complex.I * (z : ℂ)) ≠ -1 := by
            -- Use the fact that the imaginary part of z is positive
            intro h
            have h₉ := h
            simp [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.exp_re, Complex.exp_im, Complex.ext_iff, Complex.exp_re,
              Complex.exp_im] at h₉
            <;>
            (try ring_nf at h₉ ⊢) <;>
            (try norm_num at h₉ ⊢) <;>
            (try simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.exp_re, Complex.exp_im]) <;>
            (try linarith [h₅])
          exact h₈
        intro h₉
        apply h₆
        rw [add_eq_zero_iff_eq_neg] at h₉
        simpa [h₉] using h₉
      -- Use the fact that the function is smooth away from the poles
      have h₅ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (1 : ℂ) / (1 + Complex.exp (-2 * π * Complex.I * (z : ℂ)))) ℍ₀ := by
        apply (contDiffOn_const.div (contDiffOn_const.add (Complex.contDiff_exp.comp (contDiffOn_const.mul
          (contDiffOn_const.mul (contDiffOn_id : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (z : ℂ)) ℍ₀)))))).
          (by
            intro z hz
            have h₆ : (1 + Complex.exp (-2 * π * Complex.I * (z : ℂ)) : ℂ) ≠ 0 := h₄ z hz
            simp_all [h₆]
          )
      exact h₅
    exact h₃
  have h₂ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (r : ℂ) ^ 2) ℂ := by
    -- Step 2: Prove that r^2 is C^∞
    apply contDiffOn_const
  have h₃ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => Complex.exp (-(r : ℂ) ^ 2 * z.re)) ℍ₀ := by
    -- Step 3: Prove that exp(-r^2 * z.re) is C^∞ on the upper half-plane
    have h₄ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (z : ℂ).re) ℂ := by
      apply ContDiffOn.of_le (by norm_num) (by
        apply IsOpen.contDiffOn_open
        exact isOpen_Ioi.preimage (continuous_id : Continuous ((↑) : ℂ → ℂ))
        )
      <;>
      (try apply ContDiff.contDiffOn) <;>
      (try apply contDiff_id.re)
    have h₅ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (-(r : ℂ) ^ 2 : ℂ) * (z : ℂ).re) ℂ := by
      apply ContDiffOn.mul (contDiffOn_const) h₄
    have h₆ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => Complex.exp (-(r : ℂ) ^ 2 * z.re)) ℂ := by
      apply Complex.contDiff_exp.comp h₅
    have h₇ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => Complex.exp (-(r : ℂ) ^ 2 * z.re)) ℍ₀ :=
      h₆.mono (by
        -- The upper half-plane is a subset of the complex plane
        intro x hx
        trivial
      )
    exact h₇
  have h₄ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (z : ℂ) ^ 2) ℂ := by
    -- Step 4: Prove that z^2 is C^∞
    apply contDiffOn_id.pow
  -- Step 5: Combine all the parts to prove that Φ₅' r is C^∞ on the upper half-plane
  have h₅ : (Φ₅' r : ℂ → ℂ) = (fun z : ℂ => φ₀'' z * ((r : ℂ) ^ 2) * Complex.exp (-(r : ℂ) ^ 2 * z.re) * (z ^ 2)) := by
    funext z
    simp [Φ₅', φ₀'']
    <;> ring_nf
    <;> simp [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.exp_re, Complex.exp_im]
    <;> norm_num
    <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.exp_re, Complex.exp_im]
    <;> field_simp [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.exp_re, Complex.exp_im]
    <;> ring_nf
    <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.exp_re, Complex.exp_im]
    <;> norm_num
    <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.exp_re, Complex.exp_im]
  rw [h₅]
  -- Use the fact that the product of C^∞ functions is C^∞
  have h₆ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => φ₀'' z * ((r : ℂ) ^ 2) * Complex.exp (-(r : ℂ) ^ 2 * z.re) * (z ^ 2)) ℍ₀ := by
    -- Use the fact that the product of C^∞ functions is C^∞
    apply ContDiffOn.mul
    · apply ContDiffOn.mul
      · apply ContDiffOn.mul
        · exact h₁
        · exact h₂.mono (by
            -- The upper half-plane is a subset of the complex plane
            intro x hx
            trivial
          )
      · exact h₃
    · exact h₄.mono (by
        -- The upper half-plane is a subset of the complex plane
        intro x hx
        trivial
      )
  exact h₆

have h₁ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₅' r) ℍ₀ := by
    have h₂ : ContDiffOn ℂ ∞ (Φ₅' r) ℍ₀ := Φ₅'_contDiffOn_ℂ
    have h₃ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => Φ₅' r z) ℍ₀ := by
      have h₄ : ContDiffOn ℂ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => Φ₅' r z) ℍ₀ := h₂
      -- Use the fact that complex smoothness implies real smoothness
      have h₅ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => Φ₅' r z) ℍ₀ := by
        convert h₄.toReal using 1
        <;> simp [Complex.ofReal_add, Complex.ofReal_mul, Complex.ofReal_pow]
        <;> ring
        <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff]
        <;> norm_num
        <;> aesop
      -- Use the fact that complex smoothness implies real smoothness
      exact h₅
    -- Use the fact that complex smoothness implies real smoothness
    simpa using h₃
  -- Use the fact that complex smoothness implies real smoothness
  exact h₁

have h₀ : ContDiffOn ℝ ⊤ (fun z : ℂ => z⁻¹) (Set.Icc (-1 : ℂ) (1 : ℂ)) := by
    sorry
  
  have h₁ : ContDiffOn ℝ ⊤ (fun z : ℂ => (exp (-r * z) : ℂ)) ℂ := by
    have h₁ : ContDiff ℝ ⊤ (fun z : ℂ => (exp (-r * z) : ℂ)) := by
      apply ContDiff.comp (contDiff_exp) _
      apply ContDiff.mul
      · exact contDiff_const
      · exact contDiff_id
    exact h₁.contDiffOn
  
  have h₂ : ContDiffOn ℝ ⊤ (φ₀'' : ℂ → ℂ) ℂ := by
    sorry

-- The exponential function is smooth everywhere
      have h₄ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => Complex.exp (↑(-r) * z)) ℍ₀ := by
        -- Use the fact that the exponential function is smooth on all of ℂ
        have h₅ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => Complex.exp (↑(-r) * z)) Set.univ := by

          -- Use the fact that the composition of smooth functions is smooth

      apply ContDiffOn.mul h₂ h₃
    exact h₄

  have h₂ : (Φ₆' r : ℂ → ℂ) = fun z : ℂ => (φ₀'' : ℂ → ℂ) z * Complex.exp (↑(-r) * z) := rfl
  rw [h₂]
  exact h₁
